title: Forced magnetic short loop variable reluctance motor (vrm) and castelated vrm (cvrm) with negligible torque ripple
abstract: This invention is a new type of Variable Reluctance Motor (VRM) where torque ripple and noise become negligible. The method for eliminating torque ripple is to combine 4 or 6 phased motor design with a new coil configuration which ensures that the magnetic field always move in a pattern that repeat as the different phases in the motor are energized, while also ensuring that magnetic forces are balanced out so net force that pull the shaft out of center always is zero. The design can be a Castellated VRM to produce a CVRM with the more torque and the same properties as the new VRM.
claims: Claims Claims 1. Variable reluctance motor (VRM) with N phases where N > 3, and with M teeth pairs (17.7, 17.8, 17.9) where M > 2, where the VRM comprises a rotor iron (2) with rotor teeth 16, coils (4) and a stator iron (1) com- prising stator teeth (17.31, 17.32, 17.42, 17.43, 17.62, 17.1) and a shaft (3) characterized by that the adjacent teeth is grouped in teeth pairs (17.7, 17.8, 17.9) which have coils (4) belonging to the same phase wound around them arranged so the magnetic field go in a loop inside each teeth pair (17.7, 17.8, 17.9) as indicated by arrow (31), and the teeth pairs (17.7, 17.8, 17.9) are distributed evenly around the stator iron (1) for all phases so net radial forces on the shaft (3) is minimized. 2. VRM according to claim 1, where N = 3 and M = 2 creating a VRM comprising 3 phases and 12 coils (4) wound around stator teeth (17.31, 17.32) characterized by that two coils (4) from the same phase are placed around adjacent teeth pair (17.8) and that the other teeth pair (17.9) is placed on the opposite side of the stator (1) relative to the first teeth pair (17.8) for all phases. 3. VRM according to claim 1, where N = 4 and M = 2 creating a VRM comprising 4 phases and 16 coils (4) wound around stator teeth (17.42) characterized by that two coils (4) from the same phase are placed around adjacent teeth pair (17.8) and that the other teeth pair (17.9) is placed on the opposite side of the stator (1) relative to the first teeth pair (17.8) for all phases. 4. VRM according to claim 1, where N = 4 and M = 3 creating a VRM comprising 4 phases and 24 coils (4) wound around stator teeth (17.43) characterized by that two coils (4) from the same phase are placed around adjacent teeth pair (17.7) and that the teeth pairs (17.7, 17.8, 17.9) are placed 120Â° apart for all phases. 5. VRM according to claim 1, where N = 6 and M = 2 creating a VRM comprising 6 phases and 24 coils (4) wound around stator teeth (17.62) characterized by that two coils (4) from the same phase are placed around adjacent teeth pair (17.8) and that the other teeth pair (17.9) is placed on the opposite side of the stator (1) relative to the first teeth pair (17.8) for all phases. 6. VRM according one of the claims 1, 3 and 4 characterized by that a diode (32) is included in each phase, and two and two phases are connected in parallel so the current will go through one phase when the current is positive and through the other phase when the current is negative, so 4 phased VRMs can be run with a standard 2-phased VFD or be connected to a 2 phased grid. 7. VRM according to claim 1 and 6 characterized by that a diode (33) is included in each phase in the same way as in claim 6 so the 6 phased VRMs can be run with a standard 3-phased VFD or be connected to a 3 phased grid. 8. VRM according to claim 1-5 characterized by that the distance between the stator teeth (17.32, 17.42, 17.43, 17.62) in teeth pairs (17.7, 17.8, 17.9) have different internal distance (angle) than the internal distance (angle) between the rotor teeth (16) to get more space for the coils (4) and to achieve a skewing effect which can reduce torque ripple. 9. VRM according to claim 1-8 characterized by that the VRM is a CVRM where comprising 2 or more minor stator teeth (17.2) on each (major) sta- tor tooth 17.1 where the stator teeth 17.1 have configurations as described according to one of the claims 1-5 and the rotor teeth (16.2) have approx- imately the same size as the minor stator teeth (17.2). 10. VRM according to claim 1-5 and 9 characterized by skewing of the teeth defined as the stator minor teeth 17.3 having a slightly different internal distance (angle) compared to the internal distance (angle) between the rotor teeth 16.3 to achieve a skewing effect which can reduce torque ripple.
